title: Rich Peanut Oil Toast Paella
description: A rich and juicy Venezuelan dish featuring toasted bread slices cooked in peanut oil for a light yet indulgent breakfast or snack.

ingredients.name: Rice
ingredients.amount: 1 cup (uncooked) whole grain or short-grain rice, preferably medium-grained variety

instructions: Toast bread slices until golden brown and slightly crispy, about 5-7 minutes.
In a large skillet or paella dish, heat the peanut oil over medium-high heat.
Add the diced onions and sauté for about 2 minutes until translucent.
Stir in minced garlic, chopped tomatoes, and finely chopped peanuts. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes until fragrant.
Gradually add the vegetable broth or water to the skillet while stirring continuously to prevent burning. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to medium-low and stir in the rice, ensuring each grain is coated with the liquid mixture. Cover and cook for about 10 minutes.
Add the toasted bread slices evenly on top of the rice, making sure they are not touching each other too much as they will expand during cooking.
Lower the heat to low-medium, cover again, and let it simmer for another 5-8 minutes or until most liquid is absorbed by the bread and rice.
If desired, sprinkle fresh herbs over top of the dish. Season with salt and pepper before serving.
Let the toast paella rest for a few minutes after removing from heat to allow flavors to meld together.
